P0A_diagnosis.rootCause: Version-skew, NOT a missing pin you can satisfy by downgrading transformers within 5.x. peft 0.19.1 is the LATEST peft (PyPI tops out at 0.19.1 — cannot upgrade out of it). Its fused-expert merge shim peft/utils/transformers_weight_conversion.py lines 267-272 and 313-318 constructs WeightConverter(source_patterns=, target_patterns=, distributed_operation=orig.distributed_operation, quantization_operation=orig.quantization_operation, operations=). But transformers 5.6.0 / 5.10.4 / 5.11.0 / 5.12.1 ALL define WeightConverter.__init__(self, source_patterns, target_patterns, operations) — distributed_operation & quantization_operation are post-construction attributes (default None, set later in core_model_loading.py), never constructor kwargs. So the TypeError reproduces across the entire released 5.x line.
P0A_diagnosis.implication: Downgrading transformers within 5.x will NOT fix merge_and_unload. transformers 5.0.0 was inconclusive in my AST probe (WeightConverter may be shaped differently) but 5.0.0 is a large, risky downgrade for the QAT/NVFP4 train lane.
viableFixes: (1) RECOMMENDED: patch peft's 2 shim sites to construct WeightConverter(source_patterns, target_patterns, operations) then set .distributed_operation/.quantization_operation as attributes (matches the released TF5 API). Do it in an ISOLATED cert venv (not the train-lane venv) or as a monkeypatch in certify_lowram.py; upstreamable to peft.

(2) Extend certify_lowram.py's existing manual_merge fallback to merge the 32 fused-MoE expert modules directly (dequant gate_up_proj/down_proj + apply LoRA), sidestepping the peft shim entirely -> reaches 96/96.
(3) Investigate transformers 5.0.0 only if (1)/(2) are undesirable; high regression risk on the train lane.
P0A_diagnosis.notDone: No install, no patch, no re-cert performed. Awaiting cloud/owner pick between (1)/(2).
